candidates.name: Jane Hughson
candidates.notes: First elected mayor 2018, re-elected 2020, 2022, 2024. Previously served on council 1996-2002 and 2014-2018. Longtime San Marcos resident. TX State University retiree (33 years). Named as defendant in TX AG lawsuit over city sanctuary-style policies. Voted for property tax increase. Council under her leadership considered Gaza ceasefire resolution (ultimately denied).

candidates.name: Matthew Mendoza
candidates.notes: Re-elected 2025. Voted against property tax increase. Named as defendant in TX AG lawsuit over city policies. Nonpartisan city election — limited public info on positions.

candidates.name: Josh Paselk
candidates.notes: Newly elected Dec 2025 runoff, defeating incumbent Saul Gonzales with 54.4%. Term 2025-2027. Nonpartisan city election.

candidates.name: Alyssa Garza
candidates.office: City Council, Place 3 (Deputy Mayor Pro Tem)
candidates.notes: Re-elected 2024. Deputy Mayor Pro Tem. Voted FOR property tax increase. Voted to bring Gaza ceasefire resolution to a vote. Named as defendant in TX AG lawsuit over city immigration-related policies. Leans progressive.

candidates.name: Shane Scott
candidates.notes: Mayor Pro Tem. Voted AGAINST property tax increase. Was 'on the fence' on ceasefire resolution. Voted for postponement on data center project. Named in TX AG lawsuit. Appears more moderate than some council members.

candidates.name: Lorenzo Gonzalez
candidates.notes: Voted AGAINST property tax increase. Voted to bring ceasefire resolution to a vote. Mixed signals — fiscally conservative on taxes but progressive on foreign policy resolution. Named in TX AG lawsuit.

candidates.name: Amanda Rodriguez
candidates.notes: Voted FOR property tax increase. Voted to bring ceasefire resolution to a vote. Named in TX AG lawsuit. Leans progressive.

candidates.name: Ruben Becerra
candidates.notes: Serving since 2019, seeking third term. Lifetime Democrat. Chair of 2026 TX Democratic Convention. Active in multiple Democratic organizations. Family owns Gil's Broiler Restaurant and The Manske Roll Bakery in San Marcos. Married to Monica Mendez Becerra.

candidates.name: Debbie Gonzales Ingalsbe
candidates.notes: First elected 1996. First woman and first Latina county commissioner in Hays County history. Father also served as Pct 1 commissioner. Former Deputy Constable (Travis County Sheriff's Academy). Priorities: infrastructure, affordable healthcare, criminal justice reform. Ran unopposed 2024.

candidates.name: Michelle Gutierrez Cohen
candidates.notes: Elected 2022 with 60.85% of vote. 40-year Hays County resident. Doctorate in Management/Organizational Leadership. Ran Hays Latinos United (COVID resources for underserved communities). Democratic precinct chair, campaign manager for 4 campaigns. Running for Hays County Judge in 2026. Term expires 2026.

candidates.name: Morgan Hammer
candidates.notes: Elected 2024 with 52.82%. Small business owner (financial planning and real estate). TX State University McCoy School of Business graduate. Served on church leadership committees. Focuses on community values, clean water, fiscal responsibility, public safety. Precinct 3 is largest unincorporated precinct — Wimberley, Driftwood, outskirts of Kyle/San Marcos.

candidates.name: Walt Smith
candidates.notes: First elected 2018, re-elected. Republican. Represents Dripping Springs, Driftwood, portions of Buda. Running for re-election 2026 in Republican primary.

candidates.name: Kelly Higgins
candidates.notes: Elected 2022 with 53.06%. Progressive DA — does not prosecute simple cannabis possession, will not use county resources to prosecute abortion cases, established mental health courts for nonviolent offenses. Targeted under TX 'rogue prosecutors' removal law. Term expires 2026. Alfonso Salazar (D) won 2026 primary to succeed him.

candidates.name: Anthony Hipolito Jr.
candidates.notes: Elected 2024 with 52.69%. 26 years law enforcement — 23 years Austin PD (retired as Sergeant 2021). Then Hays County Sheriff's Office community outreach. Endorsed by retiring Sheriff Gary Cutler (R). Priorities: recruiting/retaining staff, managing rapid county growth, school safety, fentanyl crisis. First new Hays County sheriff in 15 years.

candidates.name: Jessica Cain

candidates.name: Sandra Sepulveda Lopez
